text: PROJECT FREEDOM DAY 1: Only 4 commercial ships crossed the Strait of Hormuz today under US Navy escort — vs 120+/day pre-war. Iran fired cruise missiles and drones at the convoy; all intercepted. US Apache helicopters sank 6 Iranian fast boats. 15,000 US service members deployed for the operation.
verificationNote: PBS, NPR, Navy Times, CENTCOM all confirmed 2 US-flagged ships transited. IranWarLive tracked only 4 total ships.

text: FUJAIRAH STRIKE: Iran launched 12 ballistic missiles, 3 cruise missiles, 4 drones at UAE. One drone penetrated UAE THAAD/Patriot layered defense, struck Fujairah Oil Industry Zone (FOIZ). Major petroleum fire. First UAE attack since April 8 ceasefire. 3 Indian nationals injured. Satellite imagery shows smoke plume visible 50km away.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, Khaleej Times, Gulf News all confirmed. VTTI oil facility named by Bloomberg.

text: IRAN INTERNAL RIFT: President Pezeshkian publicly called IRGC strikes on UAE 'madness' and 'completely irresponsible' — done without civilian government knowledge. Requested emergency meeting with Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei to halt IRGC Gulf attacks. Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf taking opposite stance: 'We have not even begun yet.'
verificationNote: Iran International confirmed Pezeshkian statement. CNN confirmed Ghalibaf quote.

text: COMPETING NARRATIVES: CENTCOM says 6 IRGC fast boats sunk targeting civilian ships. Iran claims US attacked civilian passenger boats from Khasab (Oman) to Bandar Abbas, killing 5 civilians including women. Neither claim independently verified. Business Standard reporting both versions. Key question: where did the boats come from?
verificationNote: Al Jazeera reported Iran's claim of 5 civilian deaths. CENTCOM confirmed 6 boats destroyed. Truth disputed.

text: SOUTH KOREAN SHIP: HMM Namu explosion in engine room near Hormuz. 24 crew safe. South Korea investigating — could be sea drone or mine. Seoul REBUFFS Trump's call to join Project Freedom, demands UN mandate. This mirrors Japan and EU's reluctance to participate. Coalition of the unwilling forming.
verificationNote: Jerusalem Post, Al Arabiya, SCMP confirmed attack and Seoul's rejection of Project Freedom.

text: CEASEFIRE STATUS: Effectively collapsed. Trump declines to confirm it exists. Both sides fired live rounds in Hormuz. Iran attacked a non-combatant (UAE) for first time since April 8. Araghchi heading to China for diplomatic backup. Israel-US reportedly planning new strike campaign to 'pressure' Iran. Oil at $113/bbl.
verificationNote: CNN, NPR, Bloomberg, Times of Israel — multiple sources confirm ceasefire near collapse.

text: WORLD CONDEMNS: G7, EU (Kallas), UK (Starmer), France (Macron), Germany (Merz), Canada (Carney) all issue statements condemning Iran's attacks on UAE as 'unjustifiable,' 'reckless,' 'unacceptable.' EU approving new sanctions on 19 Iranian officials. Saudi Arabia condemns 'in strongest terms.' Most unified Western response since war began.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Gulf News, The National all collected world leader condemnations.

text: MARKET IMPACT: Oil surged to $114.44/bbl May 4 (Brent) after Hormuz clashes — highest since war began. Eased to $113.24 today. WTI $104.57. AAA gas national avg now $4.48/gal (+36% from pre-war). California at $6.11/gal. IMF had already cut global growth to 3.1%. Goldman: 30% recession probability.
verificationNote: CNBC confirmed oil prices. AAA confirmed gas prices. IMF and Goldman estimates from prior reporting.

text: Iran is attempting to demonstrate 'control' over Hormuz via attacks on commercial vessels, UAE oil infrastructure, and Oman. CENTCOM reports 50+ vessels turned around since blockade started. Hezbollah claimed 8 attacks on IDF in southern Lebanon since May 3. UAE intercepted second wave of Iranian missiles/drones today.
verificationNote: Critical Threats Project (ISW/AEI) Iran Update May 4 published; CENTCOM 50+ figure confirmed by Adm. Brad Cooper.

text: DAY 2 UAE STRIKES: UAE Defence Ministry confirms second day of Iranian missiles and drones intercepted; new safety alerts. Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya Central HQ DENIES carrying out attacks despite UAE/US/witness evidence — threatens 'crushing response' if UAE acts against Iran. Separate Iran missile injures 2 foreign nationals in Oman.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, The National, Times of Israel all reported second-day attacks. Iran's denial documented in TOI live blog.

text: HEGSETH PENTAGON BRIEFING: SecDef says ceasefire 'is not over' — US stops 6 MORE Iranian port-connected ships running blockade. Calls posture 'locked and loaded.' Gen. Caine: Project Freedom is 'defensive in nature, focused in scope, and temporary in duration — protecting innocent commercial shipping from Iranian aggression.'
verificationNote: CNBC, Fox News, RedState carried Hegseth/Caine quotes from May 5 Pentagon briefing.

text: MARKETS PULL BACK: Brent eases to $111.45 (-$2.99) and WTI to $102.65 (-$3.77) on May 5 after US confirms ceasefire holds despite Day 2 UAE attacks. Still +39.7%/+36.0% vs pre-war. AAA gas hits $4.483 — highest since July 2022 (4-year high). Tennessee crossed $4.00 May 4. ~2,000 ships still stranded at Hormuz.
verificationNote: CNBC May 5 oil article, AAA Gas Prices, Action News 5 (Tennessee), Al Jazeera (stranded ships).

text: TRUMP-VATICAN RIFT WIDENS: Trump on Truth Social calls Pope Leo XIV 'weak' and accuses pontiff of 'endangering' Catholics over Iran war criticism. State Dept announces Rubio will travel to Rome and Vatican May 7-8 to meet Pope Leo and try to ease tensions. Pope had said 'God does not bless any conflict' and warned of a 'delusion of omnipotence.'
verificationNote: TIME, Washington Post, Newsmax all reported Trump's Truth Social posts and Rubio's Vatican trip.

text: RUBIO DECLARES EPIC FURY 'OVER': Secretary of State formally confirms main US combat operation in Iran has ended. 'No shooting unless we're shot at first.' US now in defensive posture — Project Freedom + naval blockade only. Meanwhile proposes UN Security Council Resolution on Hormuz freedom of navigation, co-sponsored by 5 Gulf states.
verificationNote: TIME, ABC News, CNN confirmed Rubio declaration. State Dept published resolution text.

text: IRAN LAUNCHES 'PERSIAN GULF STRAIT AUTHORITY': Formal transit permit/toll system for Hormuz. IRGC Navy issues ultimatum — ships outside designated corridor face 'decisive action.' System potentially extends IRGC monitoring zone to Fujairah. This is Iran formally institutionalizing control of world's most critical chokepoint.
verificationNote: Press TV, TASS, Maritime Executive confirmed PGSA launch and IRGC warnings.

text: TRUMP PAUSES PROJECT FREEDOM: Claims 'great progress' toward Iran deal on Truth Social. Only 2 US-flagged ships transited Day 1. Massive operation (15,000 troops, 100+ aircraft) now paused after one day. Blockade of Iranian ports remains. Pattern: escalate → pause → claim progress. No independent confirmation of deal momentum.
verificationNote: NPR, CNN, Bloomberg confirmed Project Freedom pause. No confirmation of 'great progress' in negotiations.

text: IRAN THREATENS EUROPE: Kayhan editor Shariatmadari says European military bases at US disposal 'can and must become legitimate targets.' Claims Europe has 'virtually zero capacity to withstand' Iranian attacks. Major escalation in rhetoric — extends Iranian threat doctrine beyond Gulf to NATO territory.
verificationNote: Euronews confirmed Shariatmadari op-ed in Kayhan. Kayhan's editor is directly appointed by Supreme Leader.

text: ARAGHCHI IN BEIJING: First Iran FM visit to China since war began. Meeting Wang Yi. Seeking Chinese opposition to US sanctions + emergency UNSC session. Comes days ahead of Trump's Beijing visit (May 14-15). Both sides competing for Chinese support. China already defying US sanctions on 5 refiners.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, SCMP, China MFA official announcement confirmed.

text: DAY 67 ASSESSMENT: Three critical developments — (1) Epic Fury officially ended, (2) PGSA formalizes Iran's Hormuz control, (3) Project Freedom paused after 1 day. Net effect: Iran solidifies maritime position while US shifts entirely to defensive posture. War Powers Act clock dispute unresolved. South Korea rebuffs Project Freedom.
verificationNote: Multiple sources confirm all three developments. CTP analysis synthesizes strategic implications.
